2000-12-01.title: Some in Turkey See Minefield Along Road to European Union
2000-12-01.abstract: Thorny old disputes rise up as reminders there is no guarantee of European Union membership, just when Turkey seemed to be making progress toward joining EU; trouble began on Nov 8 when Greece persuaded its 14 partners in union to add resolving division of Cyprus to list of short-term actions that Turkey must carry out before start of membership negotiations (M)

2000-12-01.title: Khatami Was 'Never a Rebel' and Has No 'Cause,' Critic Charges : Iran's Weary Would-Be Reformer
2000-12-01.abstract: TEHRAN—  Long before the outside world had heard of Mohammed Khatami, a "silent revolution" appeared to be in the making.

2000-12-01.title: Pinochet Indicted on Kidnapping Charges
2000-12-01.abstract: LIMA, Peru, Dec. 1 — An Appeals Court judge in Chile placed the country's former dictator, Gen. Augusto Pinochet, under house arrest today on charges he ordered the kidnapping, torture and murder of thousands of leftists during his 17-year rule.

2000-12-01.title: Hopes Soar as Mexico Turns a Page
2000-12-01.abstract: Many Mexicans are seen exhilarated at election of Vicente Fox Quesada as president, viewing event as a turning a page in nation's political history; five months after winning Mexico's cleanest national election, Fox, 58, will become first president in seven decades to rise to power by will of the people rather than forces of entrenched political machine; Fox has promised his country nothing less than a second Mexican revolution, bringing jobs, education, security, honest government and a measure of justice; photos (L)

2000-12-01.title: Selling Cheap 'Generic' Drugs, India's Copycats Irk Industry
2000-12-01.abstract: Article in series, Medicine Merchants, describes Indian pharmaceutical company that manufactures slightly altered versions of drugs developed and marketed by Western companies, selling them for far less; under Indian law, only manufacturing processes, not products themselves, are covered by patents; that enables Indian drug companies such as Cipla Ltd to boldy reverse-engineer best-selling drugs and sell copies cheaply; Y K Hamied, Cipla's managing director, boasts that he makes every Pfizer product; Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America (PhRMA), which represents United States industry, calls India's licensing practices 'infamous' and says experience of some American drug makers has been so negative that most companies have abandoned efforts to obtain or enforce patents in India; PhRMA study finds losses in India were $69 million a year, just for 20 common knockoff drugs; one expert puts total loss figure for India at $100 million a year; photos (L)

2000-12-02.title: Protesting Sanctions, Iraq Stops Oil Flow
2000-12-02.abstract: As part of its attempt to chip away at decade-old United Nations sanctions, Iraq has abruptly stopped exporting oil. Iraq cut off exports after failing to win authorization to divert some of its oil profits to accounts not controlled by the United Nations. Oil markets were calm in response to the action by Saddam Hussein.    Article, Page A3.

2000-12-02.title: Prayers in Jerusalem, Fatal Clashes Elsewhere
2000-12-02.abstract: First Friday of Islamic holy month of Ramadan passes without serious violence in East Jerusalem as both Palestinians and Israeli security forces show restraint; in West Bank and Gaza Strip, there are deadly street clashes; Israeli soldiers shoot and kill total of three Palestinians; photo (M)

2000-12-02.title: China's Top Justice Official Ousted, Some Say for Corruption
2000-12-02.abstract: China's minister of justice, Gao Changli, is removed from office and is reportedly under investigation for corruption; if Gao's departure involves financial improprieties, it would be another blow for Communist Party, which is reeling at scope of corruption coming to light; party is also concerned about legal battle brewing in Vancouver, Canada, over fate of China's most wanted suspect, Lai Changxing; Chinese officials accuse him of masterminding smuggling operation in Xiamen that drew hundreds of high party, police and customs officials into crime (M)

2000-12-02.title: PINOCHET'S ARREST ORDERED BY JUDGE
2000-12-02.abstract: Chilean judge formally charges Gen Augusto Pinochet in kidnappings of opponents after he overthrew Socialist government of Dr Salvador Allende in 1973, and orders him placed under house arrest; Pinochet, who is 85 and ailing, remains many legal steps away from spending time in prison; before trial can begin he will have to undergo medical tests and probably months of defense appeals; his lawyers will appeal for stay to arrest order; decision to charge Pinochet, made by Judge Juan Guzman Tapia, who under Chilean law serves as porosecutor, is first time Pinochet is charged in his homeland; it is most important legal action brought against Pinochet's 17-year dictatorship by Chile's judicial system, which until recent years remained controlled by judges appointed by military; photo (M)

2000-12-02.title: Cape Town Journal; District Six Still Is Where the Heart Is
2000-12-02.abstract: Cape Town neighborhood known as District Six is site of reunion for some of thousands of nonwhites who were forced to leave there 30 years ago by South Africa's apartheid government, which tried to turn it into whites-only community; Pres Thabo Mbeki welcomes them back, offering them chance to resettle in place that for many was their only true home; photos (M)

2000-12-03.title: Fears Voiced Over Prospect Romanian Racist May Win
2000-12-03.abstract: Romanian politicians, intellectuals and journalists evince panic at possibility that Corneliu Vadim Tudor, nationalist extremist known for racist views, may win upcoming presidential election; all political parties that lost recent elections have endorsed Ion Iliescu, former president and former Communist who received 36 percent of vote; make plea to Romanians to vote against Tudor in December 10 runoff; fret openly about Romania's chances of ever joining European Union if Tudor takes helm, given his history of racist remarks (M)

2000-12-03.title: Mexican Rebels Move Toward Peace Talks
2000-12-03.abstract: Interview with Zapatista rebel leader Subcommander Marcos, who outlines conditions under which Zapatista National Liberation Army in Chiapas, Mex, will restart negotiations with administration of new president, Vicente Fox Quesada; says rebels will agree to talks if, among other conditions, government shows good faith by removing troops from seven guerrilla strongholds; says government must also release all Zapatistas currently held in prison; photo (M)

2000-12-03.title: Brazil Miffed As Chile Shifts Trade Focus Toward U.S.
2000-12-03.abstract: Future of Brazilian-led Mercosur, world's third-largest trade bloc, is suddenly thrown into doubt by Chile's surprise announcement that it will defer request for membership and instead seek free trade agreement with US; decision by Chile, which has most open economy in Latin America, gives new momentum to proposal made by Pres Clinton in 1994 to establish Free Trade Area of the Americas by 2005 (M)

2000-12-03.title: A Chicken in Every Kyrgyzstan Pot
2000-12-03.abstract: Residents of Madi, Kyrgyzstan, are among beneficiaries of UN program that, for first time, is bringing microcredit to former Soviet lands of Central Asia; after decades isolated under Communism, with credit alien concept, few of poorest villagers are learning benefits brought by even small loans that their own banks and leaders are unable to furnish; foreign benefactors hope that in process villagers will acquire skills needed to build not only businesses but also society free of old totalitarian ways; map; photos (M)

2000-12-03.title: Swiss Report Says Gypsies Were Turned Back in Nazi Era
2000-12-03.abstract: New report by historians concludes Swiss officials systematically expelled Gypsies or turned them away at border during World War II, returning many to likely death in Nazi Germany; is part of examination of nation's wartime actions by independent historians' commission that found Switzerland refused to accept many Jews fleeing Hitler's Germany, leading to almost certain death for thousands (M)

2000-12-04.title: Online Pioneer Nifty on Its Guard : Bracing for the Internet Shakeout
2000-12-04.abstract: TAKETSUNE Watanabe,—  The president of Nifty Corp., Japan's biggest Internet service provider, doesn't fit the Western model of the caffeine-charged, goateed-and-pierced dot-com boss.

2000-12-04.title: Indonesia Cracks Down on Separatists in Irian Jaya
2000-12-04.abstract: Severe Indonesian crackdown on separatists in Irian Jaya has left at least 10 people dead; Pres Abdurrahman Wahid sent thousands of heavily armed troops to province, where residents marked anniversary of 1961 declaration of independence; dozens of pro-independence leaders are under arrest and security forces enforce new ban on flying separatist flag; human rights groups and some political activists score crackdown; Indonesians fear clashes between military and residents could grow; map; photo (M)

2000-12-04.title: Banana Island Journal; Lovely Spot, Ripe for Tourists! About That War ...
2000-12-04.abstract: banana island journal: lovely spot, ripe fo

2000-12-04.title: A Fresh Gold Mine in Prepaid Phone Cards
2000-12-04.abstract: MANILA—  Angelino Gabon, 23, is an apprentice goldsmith whose salary, he laments, is something less than 24-karat.

2000-12-04.title: Iraq Says It Will Resume Oil Exports
2000-12-04.abstract: Iraq says it will resume oil exports to fulfill existing contracts under oil-for-food program, backing away from halt it called in dispute with United Nations over pricing; halt did not disrupt oil markets (M)

2000-12-05.title: A Fashion Bad Boy Defects to Gucci
2000-12-05.abstract: PARIS—  Alexander McQueen, a self-styled fashion bad boy, was poached Monday from under the nose of the luxury mogul Bernard Arnault, adding to the bad blood between Mr. Arnault's company, LVMH, and Gucci Group.

2000-12-05.title: Infectious Diseases Rising Again in Russia
2000-12-05.abstract: Russia's political turmoil, its economic crisis and its new freedoms have been accompanied by a surge in infectious diseases. Tuberculosis is flooding the country, producing what some authorities are calling the largest outbreak in the world of one of medicine's most ominous problems, drug-resistant TB. Rates of other infections, including hepatitis, syphilis and AIDS, are skyrocketing. Health experts worry that if the rising rates of infectious diseases continue unabated, Russia itself may turn into an epidemiologic pump, sending infectious diseases into the rest of the world. Freedom's Toll:  Science Times, F1.

2000-12-05.title: Venezuelan Voters Approve Removal of Labor Leaders
2000-12-05.abstract: Venezuelan voters decisively approve referendum ousting labor union leaders, who are sympathetic to opposition parties; outcome paves way for single new government-dominated workers' federation; Pres Hugo Chavez ordered vote as part of 'peaceful social revolution' he has promised (M)

2000-12-05.title: Rightist Squads in Colombia Beating the Rebels
2000-12-05.abstract: United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia, right-wing paramilitary group, is directly engaging guerrillas, winning wide swaths of territory and gaining support from country's landowners, businessmen and coca growers; photos; map; militias have tightened hold on northern provinces and expanded into other regions, through intimidation, massacres and, increasingly, direct confrontations (M)

2000-12-05.title: Dr. Cyril Clarke, 93, British Rh Disease Geneticist, Dies
2000-12-05.abstract: Dr Cyril Clarke, British physician credited with halting fatal newborn disease stemming from Rh factor incompatability, dies at age 93; photo (M)

2000-12-06.title: Mexico Plans To Unravel Illegal Web Of Wiretaps
2000-12-06.abstract: Adolfo Aguilar Zinser, head of Mexico's new national security council, says he will try to unravel illegal web of wiretaps and other government surveillance that has long been used to intimidate opposition leaders, political parties, media and union leaders; Zinser says new government of Pres Vicente Fox still does not know extent of wiretapping network it has inherited (M)

2000-12-06.title: Soviet Hymn Is Back, Creating Much Discord
Headaches Pile Up on Ukraine Leader
2000-12-06.abstract: Pres Leonid Kuchma of Ukraine is coming under increasing pressure as he faces multiple crises; first is monumental task on Dec 15 of closing last reactor at Chernobyl nuclear power station, shutdown that threatens further power cuts for millions of Ukrainians; then, International Monetary Fund is holding back on certifying Ukraine as creditworthy even as Pres Vladimir V Putin of Russia is calling for Ukraine to pay off $1.5 billion debt; and finally, there is furor over opposition party's public release of tape recording supposedly featuring Kuchma and top aides hurling curses and threats at muckraking journalist Georgy Gongadze, who disappeared on Sept 16 (M)

2000-12-06.title: World Briefing
2000-12-06.abstract: EUROPE    EUROPEAN UNION: COHEN'S WARNING  Defense Secretary William S. Cohen warned the European Union not to set up a military structure that could compete with NATO. The 15-nation union has vowed to establish a 60,000-member force by 2003, a move the United States supports on the conditions that NATO remain the primary guarantor of European security and do the planning for both groups. But France has insisted on a more autonomous role for the European Union. If the Europeans insist on setting up a rival planning structure, Mr. Cohen said, ''NATO could become a relic of the past.''    Michael R. Gordon (NYT)

2000-12-06.title: America to Press Security in Distant Ports
2000-12-06.abstract: US Navy officials say that United States plans to negotiate new security arrangements with every country that American warships visit, establishing tougher measures meant to thwart terrorist attacks like one against destroyer Cole in Yemen in October (M)

2000-12-07.title: A White Party Emerges to Challenge the A.N.C. in South Africa
2000-12-07.abstract: Democratic Alliance, predominantly white political party in South Africa that has aggressively courted black voters, seems poised to win quarter of popular vote in local elections; preliminary count suggests party would emerge as strongest challenger to governing African National Congress since apartheid ended six years ago; ANC has easily maintained its national dominance, and recent polls suggest that most blacks still stand behind party of Nelson Mandela; but latest election results make it clear that Democratic Alliance is serious opposition party (M)

2000-12-07.title: Europe's Spreading Food Scare : Untangling the Deadly 'Mad Cow' Mystery
2000-12-07.abstract: PARIS—  Nobody knows how it started. Nobody knows how it will end. Nobody knows how many people eventually will die from it. Those are among the frightening mysteries scientists are discovering about "mad cow" disease, or BSE, the bovine form of transmissible spongiform encephalopathy.

2000-12-07.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-12-07.abstract: THE AMERICAS    MEXICO: SEEKING EX-MAYOR'S EXTRADITION -- The government wants to extradite the former mayor of Mexico City, now seeking asylum in Nicaragua, on charges he embezzled $45 million while in office from 1994 to 1997. The former mayor, Oscar Espinosa, left, who also served as a minister of tourism and as a top campaign aide to former President Ernesto Zedillo, says he is a victim of political persecution. Nicaraguan officials appear likely to honor Mexico's request.    Tim Weiner (NYT)

2000-12-07.title: Shakespeare's Mother, Mary, Gets a New Address
2000-12-07.abstract: Custodians of William Shakespeare's legacy are embroiled in comedy of errors regarding birthplace of Shakespeare's mother, Mary Arden; for decades guides have directed tourists to cottage in Wilmcote, but new research has shown she was born in house 30 yards down the road; photos (M)

2000-12-07.title: High Profits Open Way to Black Market In Animal Feed
2000-12-07.abstract: BRUSSELS—  European Union farm ministers have decreed a temporary ban on meat and bone meal in an effort to halt "mad cow" disease, but experience in Belgium with illegal hormones indicates that the policy is unlikely to work consistently.

2000-12-08.title: South Koreans Grow Impatient With U.S. Stand on '50 Massacre
2000-12-08.abstract: Many Soth Koreans seem to be losing patience with US as officials from both countries fail to agree on joint statement about alleged murder of refugees at No Gun Ri in 1950, shortly after Korean War began (S)

2000-12-08.title: Europeans, and Protesters, Meet on the Riviera
2000-12-08.abstract: Protesters clash with police in Nice as European Union leaders open summit meeting by promising to devise structure for accepting new countries as members (S)

2000-12-08.title: Strife in the North Rattles Stable Mozambique
2000-12-08.abstract: Northern Mozambique, where much of 16-year civil war has raged, reflects unresolved antagonisms that have shadowed country since 1992 peace accord; photos; map; at least 40 people were killed in recent clashes with police and at least 80 more died in prison, apparently of suffocation; Pres Joaquim Chissano invites Renamo guerrilla leader Afonso Dhlakama to first face-to-face meeting in about two years (M)

2000-12-08.title: New Serbian Coalition Reaches Agreement on Sharing Power
2000-12-08.abstract: Leaders of 18-party coalition that toppled Slobodan Milosevic agree on sharing power in new Serb government; Zoran Djindjic is in line for prime minister, although strains do exist between him and Pres Vojislav Kostunica (S)

2000-12-08.title: Colombian's Peace Pursuit: Part Carrot, Part Stick
2000-12-08.abstract: News analysis on faltering peace efforts in Colombia and Pres Andres Pastrana's decision to offer FARC rebels another concession, allowing them to extend hold on large swath of territory that government ceded to them in 1998; photo of damage from rebel car bombing in Granada (M)

2000-12-09.title: London Journal; At Lunch, Prince Philip Outwits the Yankee Press
2000-12-09.abstract: Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh, is brusque and even dismissive at times during a lunch with American reporters in Britain, but he deftly thwarts all their efforts to get him to say something ill considered; photo (M)

2000-12-09.title: Russian Deputies Restore Soviet National Anthem
2000-12-09.abstract: Russia's Parliament votes to restore Soviet national anthem as state hymn--its words to be rewritten--and to bring back Soviet red banner--without hammer and sickle--as official flag of armed forces; legislation, which was submitted by Pres Vladimir V Putin to the dismay of many but not all liberals and intellectuals, retains 300-yaer-old Russian tricolor of white, blue and red as state flag and czarist era double-headed eagle as national seal; photo (M)

2000-12-09.title: Inquiry Faults the Cole's Captain and Crew
2000-12-09.abstract: Navy inquiry finds that Cmdr Kirk S Lippold, captain of the destroyer Cole, and its crew failed to follow strict security procedures before a skiff packed with explosives attacked it in Yemen in Ocober; adds, however, that it is not clear whether attack would have been averted if Cole's crew had followed its own security procedures to the letter; photo (M)

2000-12-09.title: 10 Die as Violence Surges on Palestinian 'Day of Rage'
2000-12-09.abstract: Israeli security forces kill seven Palestinians, and Palestinian gunmen kill three Israelis in one of deadliest days of violence in West Bank and East Jerusalem since outbreak of current Palestinian uprising; violence comes in 'day of rage' called by the mainstream Palestinian movment Fatah and Islamic groups to mark start of first Palestinian uprising, which began in 1987 and ended after 1993 Oslo peace accord; photo; map (M)

2000-12-09.title: New Governor of Chiapas Promises, 'No More Bloodshed'
2000-12-09.abstract: Pablo Salazar Mendiguchia is inaugrated as governor of Chiapas, Mexico, and he promises to create justice system that will protect people from terror and help end layers of religious, political and ethnic conflicts that erupt in violence; orders state's attorney general to review cases of all those who say they are Zapatista political prisoners and to suspend sentences of those who were charged with political crimes; Pres Vicente Fox reiterates his commitment to pursuing peace; sends to Congress series of changes aimed at granting limited autonomy to indigenous communities; photo; map (M)

2000-12-10.title: In Battling Gazans, Israelis Sow Seeds of Hate
2000-12-10.abstract: Palestinian population in Gaza grows steadily angrier and voices of moderation get ever quieter, as long period of violence produces mounting toll of death, injury and property damage; many Israelis believe their government has used restraint in confronting violent uprising manipulated by Palestinian leadership; but Palestinians do not see any restraint; photo; map (M)

2000-12-10.title: A U.N. Aide Says Taliban Is Reducing Poppy Crop
2000-12-10.abstract: United Nations' top antinarcotics official says that Taliban government in Afghanistan appears to be succeeding in slowing significantly cultivation of opium poppies for first time since radical Islamic movement seized power four years ago (M)

2000-12-10.title: Effort to Repair Armenia and Azerbaijan Ties
2000-12-10.abstract: International negotiators, in effort to resolve smoldering conflict over mountainous enclave of Nagorno-Karabakh, are trying to assemble aid package that would help make peace palatable to bitterly opposed peoples of Armenia and Azerbaijan; negotiatiors secure what is portrayed as important promise from Turkey to aid both countries if peace agreement is reached; map (M)

2000-12-10.title: Mixed Verdict From Manila's Mean Streets in President's Trial
2000-12-10.abstract: Impeachment trial of Pres Joseph Estrada grinds on in Philippine Senate, while in the streets, Manila's poorest residents have mixed reactions to corruption charges against man who was swept into power as hero of the poor (M)

2000-12-10.title: A Widening Political Rift in Ivory Coast
2000-12-10.abstract: There is widening political rift in Ivory Coast; six weeks after stunning popular revolt, religious and ethnic divisions unleashed by fall of military junta have widened dangerously, and there appears to be no end in sight to political stalemate paralyzing this onetime bastion of stability in West Africa; Muslims and Christians killed each other this week in riots that led to state of emergency; for first time, there are calls for secession of Muslim north from Christian south; photo of Pres Laurent Gbagbo (M)

2000-12-11.title: Shadowed By Bitterness, Vote Is Held In Ivory Coast
2000-12-11.abstract: Ivory Coast holds parliamentary elections; country's fractured politics depresses turnout in largely Christian south and shuts down voting in several cities in Muslim north; photo; map (S)

2000-12-11.title: Former Chief Of Romania Retakes Office Over Rightist
2000-12-11.abstract: Romanian voters, in what experts call a showing of political maturity, give former Pres Ion Iliescu another term in office, rejecting ultranationalist, Corneliu Vadim Tudor, by large margin; photo (M)

2000-12-11.title: Bogota Journal; An Ugly-Duckling Face Wins TV-Watchers' Hearts
2000-12-11.abstract: Success of Colombian soap opera I Am Betty the Ugly, with its frumpy, klutzy protagonist, is particularly surprising because of nation's stress on beauty; show has drawn Colombians by millions to tale of noble and loyal heroine who gets ahead through brains and hard work; shows is also popular in 14 other nations; photos (M)

2000-12-11.title: Opening Campaign, Netanyahu Invokes 'Will of the Nation'
2000-12-11.abstract: Former Israeli Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu announces intention to recapture Likud Party leadership and office of prime minister; says Prime Min Ehud Barak should not be allowed to frustrate will of nation by blocking Netanyahu's ability to run for office; says he will work to brush aside political and legal obstacles to his candidacy placed in his path by Barak's recent resignation, which he calls 'cynical political trick;' under law for special elections, called by Barak, field of candidates is limited to members of Parliament; Netanyahu quit seat and Likud leadership day Barak overwhelmingly defeated him for prime minister in 1999; photos (M)

2000-12-11.title: Europeans Approve Expansion Of Union
2000-12-11.abstract: European Union leaders reach agreement on package of changes intended to restructure bloc's decision-making institutions and prepare union to accept more than a dozen new members in next decade or so; biggest achievement is reweighting of voting, which increases say of uion's biggest countries--Germany, France, Italy and Britain (M)

2000-12-12.title: European Union Reform: After 5 Days, a Yawn
2000-12-12.abstract: News analysis of summit meeting of European leaders in Nice, France; meeting is billed as a gathering that would overhaul European Union's unwieldy decision-making structures; it is clear that no such streamlining had taken place; photo (M)

2000-12-12.title: Kim Tells Nobel Audience Of Need for Korean Unity
2000-12-12.abstract: Excerpts from South Korean Pres Kim Dae Jung's acceptance speech for his Nobel Peace Prize, awarded for his longstanding campaign for human rights and reconciliation with North Korea; photo (M)

2000-12-12.title: Syria Frees About 50 of Its Lebanese Prisoners
2000-12-12.abstract: Syria frees about 50 Lebanese political prisoners, after refusing to tell their families or their government whether they were dead or alive; gesture is meant to placate increasingly vocal anti-Syria movement in Lebanon (S)

2000-12-12.title: Burmese Junta May Be Ready To Release Top Opponent
2000-12-12.abstract: Military dictatorship in Myanmar signals that it might soon release Daw Aung San Suu Kyi, leader of country's democratic opposition and winner of Nobel Peace Prize who has been under house arrest since September (M)

2000-12-12.title: Leaders Tinker but Fail to Find a Shared Goal : Europe Without Direction
2000-12-12.abstract: NICE—  In opening the way for the European Union's enlargement into eastern Europe, its leaders have come up with a treaty that rejiggers details of its organization but fails one more time to bring the EU a sense of unified power or direction.

2000-12-13.title: The Chileans V. Pinochet
2000-12-13.abstract: News analysis of legal wrangling over fate of former Chilean dictator Augusto Pinochet; legal drama and Pres Ricardo Lagos's degree of success in negotiating arrangement with armed forces for future human rights trials are likely to cast long shadow over country's future; will define independence of civilian government and judiciary and show once and for all whether military is ready to leave politics to others (M)

2000-12-13.title: Mexican President Praises Migrant 'Heroes'
2000-12-13.abstract: Mexico's Pres Vicente Fox begins two-day tour of US border areas to reach out to Mexicans heading home for Christmas holidays and to honor campaign pledge to honor Mexicans living in US; lauds migrants as 'heroes' (S)

2000-12-13.title: Denying All, Milosevic Opens Campaign for Political Comeback
2000-12-13.abstract: Slobodan Milosevic, former Yugoslav president, gives first television interview since forced to relinquish power, in which he denies accusations of misrule and of falsifying election results; interview marks start of campaign for political comeback and for his Socialist Party in Serbian Parliament elections on December 23 (M)

2000-12-13.title: Pressure Is Again Emerging To Free Jonathan Pollard
2000-12-13.abstract: Pres Clinton, who nears end of tenure in office, faces new round of pressure to free Jonathan Pollard, convicted spy whose life sentence has become battleground between Jewish leaders and intelligence officials; administration officials say Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak recently raised issue with Clinton, who restated official position that he would review issue along with other clemency requests; say Israel and Jewish leaders will likely press efforts; senior law enforcement and intelligence officials seen just as likely to maintain strong opposition to freeing Pollard, civilian naval intelligence analyst sentenced to life in prison as spy in 1987; Pollard claims he was punished too severely since he gave information US should have been supplying its ally; photo (M)

2000-12-13.title: Palermo Shows Off as a Cleaned-Up Mafia Capital
2000-12-13.abstract: Palermo, Sicily, which that less than decade ago was viewed as unenviable world capital of organized crime, has recast itself as epicenter of international battle against mafia; will host three-day UN conference on combating organized crime, event that is giving it chance to convince world that it has shaken loose murderous grip of Cosa Nostra; has been praised by Sec Gen Kofi Annan for efforts to overcome organized criminality that has tarnished its image for many years; photos (M)

2000-12-14.title: Armthorpe Journal; Illness That Haunts Europe: One Family's Story
2000-12-14.abstract: Article on rising incidence in Europe of Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease, fatal human form of mad cow disease; 85 Britons, including three linked to village of Armthorpe, have contracted disease since first diagnosis in mid-1990's; death of 19-year-old resident Matthew Middleton from disease discussed; map; photo (M)

2000-12-14.title: As Charges Mount, Heat Increases For Chirac
2000-12-14.abstract: Political problems mount for French Pres Jacques Chirac, as new information surfaces in press suggesting he may have been major player in corruption scheme when he was mayor of Paris; his conservative party allegedly received money from companies that did business with city (M)

2000-12-14.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-12-14.abstract: ASIA    CHINA: PLACES OF WORSHIP DESTROYED -- Local officials and newspapers in the southeast city of Wenzhou said hundreds of unauthorized churches and temples in the surrounding region had been demolished or closed this month. Officials say they are fighting dangerous and illegal superstitions in this area, where unregistered Christian groups as well as unapproved ancestor worship and traditional sects have flourished.    Erik Eckholm (NYT)    TAIWAN: TRAVEL BAN EASED -- Taiwan announced rules, effective Jan. 1, to permit limited travel and trade between its outer islands of Quemoy and Matsu and mainland China, using only Taiwanese boats. This would be the first easing of Taiwan's longstanding ban on direct links to the mainland, but is far short of the broad opening of commercial ties both sides say they want. Beijing has refused to negotiate any issues with Taiwan unless it accepts the ''one China'' principle, and it is not clear if the mainland will cooperate with the new rules.   Erik Eckholm (NYT)

2000-12-14.title: Czechs Agree to Let Outside Experts Inspect Atomic Power Plant
2000-12-14.abstract: Austria and Czech Republic, with mediation of European Uniuon, sign agreement to resolve dispute over new Czech nuclear power plant 30 miles from Austrian border; plant, which began testing in October, will be evaluated for safety and environmental effects by team of experts chosen by European Union; will not operate at commercial levels or produce electricity until report is completed, probably in May or June (M)

2000-12-14.title: George W. Bush:Who America's Next President Is and Where He Stands

2000-12-15.title: Silk Workers In Standoff With Beijing Over Union
2000-12-15.abstract: Workers at ailing state-owned Funing County Silk Mill in Jiangsu Province are in tense standoff with authorities over their daring proposal to form worker-run trade union in place of official body that they say has failed to defend their interests; simmering dispute involving corruption and unpaid allowances and pensions is typical of China's older state industries, but more open than usual (M)

2000-12-15.title: First for a Top Mexican Aide: Facing Trial in $45 Million Theft
2000-12-15.abstract: Oscar Espinosa Villareal, latest in long line of Mexican politicians turned fugitive from justice, is in Nicaraguan jail, awaiting extradition; former Mexico City mayor will likely be charged with stealing as much as $4 million city's treasury, and would be highest-ranking official ever to face criminal trial in country (M)

2000-12-15.title: Chirac, on TV, Denies Knowing Anything of Kickback Schemes
2000-12-15.abstract: French Pres Jacques Chirac vehemently denies knowing anything about kickback schemes to raise money for his party while he was mayor of Paris, although he says it was widely understood that all parties had relied on illicit fundraising up until 1995; gives rare one-hour television interview in which he sounds alternately combative and outraged (M)

2000-12-15.title: Clinton, Winding Up Trip, Tells Developed Nations Not to Forget the World's Poor
2000-12-15.abstract: Pres Clinton concludes what may be his last overseas trip as president with plea to developed countries not to forget the poor in age of high technology and globalization; speaks at University of Warwick, introduced by his close friend, Prime Min Tony Blair; Clinton, his wife and daughter Chelsea earlier have tea and cookies with Queen Elizabeth at Buckingham Palace (M)

2000-12-15.title: Fearing Terrorism, U.S. Keeps Consulates in Turkey Closed
2000-12-15.abstract: US consulates in Istanbul and Adana will remain closed indefinitely because of fears of potential terrorist attacks, although officials say there have been no specific threats against American sites in Turkey (S)

2000-12-16.title: London Journal; 'Nice Little Run' of 'Mousetrap' Hits 20,000 Tonight
2000-12-16.abstract: Production of Agatha Christie murder mystery Mousetrap is set for its 20,000th performance in London's West End; play opened on Nov 25, 1952, has been seen by more than 10 million people, and has never missed a performance; photos (London Journal) (M)

2000-12-16.title: U.N. Economic Panel to Study Ways to Help World's Have-Nots
New Europe's Changing Landscape Strains French-German Ties
2000-12-16.abstract: France and Germany schedule summit meeting in attempt to resolve sharp differences that are straining close bond between them that has been central to development of European Union; scheduling of special talks reflects Germany's desire for enlarged role in European Union commensurate with its post-cold-war power as France, fearing becoming marginalized, clings to old equilibrium between the two states (M)

2000-12-16.title: Sides in Mideast Conflict Seem to Edge Toward Resuming Talks
2000-12-16.abstract: Israelis and Palestinians appear to edge closer to resuming peace negotiations after meeting between Foreign Min Shlomo Ben-Ami and Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat; even as diplomatic activity accelerates, Israeli soldiers kill six Palestinians in new surge of violence in West Bank and Gaza, raising conflict's toll to more than 320 lives; photo (M)

2000-12-16.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-12-16.abstract: EUROPE    TURKEY: OCALAN PLEA  The European Court of Human Rights will hear an appeal brought against Turkey by the jailed Kurdish separatist leader Abdullah Ocalan, 51, and his lawyers said he would make a broad defense of his actions on historical and political grounds. The court said he would be tried next year before 17 judges.    Douglas Frantz (NYT)

2000-12-17.title: In Chiapas, a Suffering With No End in Sight
2000-12-17.abstract: Members of Zapatista National Liberation Army and their supporters continue to live in fear and misery in Mexico's Chiapas state even as Pres Vicent Fox takes some steps to meet their demands; hopelessness and hunger overwhelm townships like San Pedro Polho, where many of the people are refugees driven from their land after attacks by pro-government paramilitary groups; photo; map (M)

2000-12-17.title: Christabel Leighton-Porter, A Comic Stripper, Dies at 87
2000-12-17.abstract: Christabel Leighton-Porter, who became sweetheart of British troops in World War II as model for comic strip heroine who was forever shedding her clothes, dies at age 87 (M)

2000-12-17.title: A Sheik's Lessons: Hatred for the West
2000-12-17.abstract: From his base in northern Yemen, one of the Arab world's most militantly anti-Western Islamic clerics runs study centers for thousands of Muslim men. The cleric, Sheik Muqbel bin Hadi al-Wadie, denies that the work includes weapons training, saying his movement is a peaceful one that spreads the concepts of Islam. The Koran, he says, is more powerful than tanks.    But the F.B.I. has long considered the centers to be incubators for the holy war that Osama bin Laden has declared against the United States. And in a region where the United States is still considered ''the Great Satan,'' the best advice to Americans would be: stay away.    Article, Page 36.

2000-12-17.title: In Africa, A Mea Culpa For Apartheid Tests Whites
2000-12-17.abstract: Some of South Africa's leading white intellectuals and civic figures launch campaign urging fellow whites to acknowledge that every white South African was, and still is, a beneficiary of old apartheid system and to make commitment to combat injustice and inequality; mea culpa is criticized and derided by many white South Africans (M)

2000-12-17.title: Indonesians Flee Wars, Only to Find More Woes
2000-12-17.abstract: More than a million Indonesians have fled their homes in several regions hit by separatist violence, religious strife and natural disasters; mosques, schools, public buildings and makeshift shelters in which they have taken refuge lack food and medicine to treat the diarrhea, measles, eye infections, flu and other ailments spreading quickly among them (M)

2000-12-18.title: Millions in Sudan Facing Food and Water Shortages
2000-12-18.abstract: United Nations World Food Program reports more than 3.2 million people in Sudan are facing serious food and water shortages because of combined disruptions of civil war and widening drought (S)

2000-12-18.title: Panel Backs Stronger Rules For Some Food
2000-12-18.abstract: Blue-ribbon biotechnology committee formed by US and European Union will reportedly recommend that Washington strengthen regulation of genetically modified foods and move toward mandatory labeling (S)

2000-12-18.title: Barak and Arafat Will Send Aides to Talks in Washington
2000-12-18.abstract: Israeli and Palestinian officials, for first time since violence erupted in late September, are dropping conditions for resuming peace negotiations and are beginning to talk anew; representatives for both sides will travel to Washington on December 18; Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat says he is open to substantive session with Prime Min Ehud Barak if groundwork is fully prepared; US and Israel are trying to imbue Palestinians with sense of deadline pressure as Pres Clinton's days in office are numbered, and Prime Min Barak is competing for re-election against all odds; photo (M)

2000-12-18.title: 2015 Outlook: Enough Food, Scarce Water, Porous Borders
2000-12-18.abstract: Report by US intelligence community offers sweeping projection of what world will look like in 2015; Global Trends 2015, unclassified report, concludes that issues like availability of water and food, changes in population and spread of information and disease will increasingly affect national security of US; its findings detailed (M)

2000-12-18.title: Seagram Unit Is Expected To Be Sold
2000-12-18.abstract: Diageo PLC and its partner, Pernod Ricard SA, are reportedly poised to buy Seagram Company's wine and beverage business for about $8.2 billion (S)

2000-12-19.title: 40 Miles From Mexico City, Volcano Spews Rocks and Ash
2000-12-19.abstract: Popocatepetl volcano 40 miles from Mexico City erupts with plumes of lava, hours after tens of thousands of people living in vicinity ignored government's pleas to evacuate; eruption is potentially biggest in centuries (S)

2000-12-19.title: Jakarta Journal; It's Ramadan. School Is Out. Quick, the Earplugs!
2000-12-19.abstract: Noisy celebrations marking Muslim holy month of Ramadan in Jakarta, Indonesia, sets off widespread complaints from Muslims and non-Muslims alike; cacaphony, caused by youths banging on drums and setting off firecrackers and noise makers at all hours, is worst in recent memory; observance is perhaps one time of year when Indonesia, which has never been concerned with following Islam to letter, tries to act like traditional Muslim state; photo (M)

2000-12-19.title: World Briefing
2000-12-19.abstract: EUROPE    RUSSIA: A TOP CHECHEN REPORTED SLAIN -- The Kremlin said Russian forces, in a special operation, had ''annihilated'' the brother of Shamil Basayev, the top commander of rebel forces in Chechnya. The brother, Shirvani Basayev, has also been a leader of the rebels' separatist movement and took part in peace talks with Russia in 1996. A spokesman for the rebel forces declined comment. Russian announcements of similar triumphs have sometimes turned out to be false.   Michael Wines (NYT)

2000-12-19.title: Putin Pays a Visit to Canada, Winning Support on Missile Issue
2000-12-19.abstract: Russian Pres Vladimir Putin visits Canada, where he wins support for strengthening 1972 Antiballistic Missile Treaty that Russians see as blocking proposed US national missile defense system (M)

2000-12-19.title: Burmese Sales To the PX Are Provoking Rights Protest
2000-12-19.abstract: Army and Air Force Exchange Service, Pentagon agency that runs stores on American military bases, imported $138,290 in clothing made in Myanmar at time when Clinton administration had banned new investments in country; Congressional critics and human rights groups say while such purchases are not illegal they violate spirit of administration's economic sanctions (M)

2000-12-20.title: Annan in Touch With Bush and Team
2000-12-20.abstract: United Nations Sec Gen Kofi Annan says that during past week he has spoken with Pres-elect George W Bush as well as secretary of state-designate Gen Colin L Powell and Condoleezza Rice, who will become national security adviser; Annan says he feels confident from these conservations that United Nations will benefit from support of Bush administration (S)

2000-12-20.title: Clinton Trip to North Korea Is Mired in Transition Politics
2000-12-20.abstract: Clinton administration is deciding whether to push ahead toward agreement with North Korea that would shut down its missile program; to complete deal, Clinton would have to go to North Korea in next 35 days and meet with Kim Jong Il; but Pres-elect George W Bush's foreign policy advisers, although staying publicly neutral, are saying in private that Clinton visit would amount to grandstanding; if Clinton wins enforceable agreement, it would undermine one of chief rationales that Bush has put forward for United States national missile defense program (M)

2000-12-20.title: Military Hospital to Examine Pinochet, Chilean Court Rules
2000-12-20.abstract: Chilean appeals court rules that former dictator Gen Augusto Pinochet should be examined in military hospital before facing trial on charges of kidnapping and murder; human rights lawyers had argued that Pinochet should be examined in civilian hospital, where he would recieve more objective examination to see whether he is mentally competent to stand trial (M)

2000-12-20.title: Macao Journal; If Life's a Crap Game, the Isle of Casinos Is Smiling
2000-12-20.abstract: Macao welcomes Pres Jiang Zemin on visit marking its first year under Chinese sovereignty; Portuguese left Macao riven by gang violence, drained by recession and demoralized by decades of neglect, and today it is cleaner, inside and out; downside of law and order introduced by the Chinese is a growing intolerance of dissent; photos; map (M)

2000-12-20.title: 550-Seat Plane Will Be Built; U.S. Warns Against Subsidies : Airbus Superjumbo Faces Trade Storm
2000-12-20.abstract: PARIS—  Airbus Industrie's project to build the world's largest passenger jet took off Tuesday into the unfriendly skies of a looming trans-Atlantic trade storm.

2000-12-21.title: Pakistanis And Indians Make Moves For Less Strife
2000-12-21.abstract: India and Pakistan announce steps to reduce tensions along de facto border in Kashmir; Indian Prime Min Atal Behari Vajpayee extends unilateral cease-fire and opens 'exploratory steps' on peace talks; Paksitani army sets partial withdrawal along Line of Control (M)

2000-12-21.title: Gates Foundation's $15 Million Seeks Drugs for African Diseases
2000-12-21.abstract: Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation gives $15 million to develop drugs to treat sleeping sickness and leishmaniasis, parasitic diseases that kill thousands of Africans each year; Dr Richard R Tidwell and his team at University of North Carolina, along with others, will test compound DB-289, which shows promise of killing parasite (S)

2000-12-21.title: In Final Fight, Weary Envoy Counts Ticks On U.N. Clock
2000-12-21.abstract: Richard C Holbrooke, American ambassador to United Nations, is running out of diplomatic patience after more than 16 months in thankless job of refereeing between hostile Congress and a lot of angry countries; another deadline passes for deciding whether United States will get a reduction in dues its pays to both regular and peacekeeping budgets; gavel comes down on Assembly session on Dec 22 (M)

2000-12-21.title: High Court Voids Charges For Pinochet; Sets New Date
2000-12-21.abstract: Chilean Supreme Court dismisses kidnapping charges against Gen Augusto Pinochet; but court orders that Pinochet be interrogated in 20 days, so he might be re-arrested and charged again (M)

2000-12-21.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-12-21.abstract: ASIA    AFGHANISTAN: TALIBAN ACTS OVER SANCTIONS -- The ruling Taliban responded to tough new United Nations sanctions by pulling out of United Nations-mediated talks on ending Afghanistan's civil war and refusing any handover to the United States of the terrorism suspect Osama bin Laden. The Taliban also ordered an immediate boycott of products from the United States and Russia.    (AP)

2000-12-22.title: Quicavi Journal; A Rich Life of the Mind Makes a Hard Life Easier
2000-12-22.abstract: Old fishing village of Quicavi, Chile, has long tradition of chilling ghost stories and rich fantasy life from time when clandestine Indian warlocks used curses and potions agianst Spanish landowners; some of latter-day tales recounted; photos; map (Quicavi Journal) (M)

2000-12-22.title: Ecuador Decides to Wean Military From Oil Riches
2000-12-22.abstract: Ecuador's Congress strips armed forces of share of government oil royalties, one of army's main sources of income (S)

2000-12-22.title: Colombia Rebels Offer to Free 45 Hostages
2000-12-22.abstract: Colombia's second-largest leftist guerrilla group, National Liberation Army, announces intention of releasing 45 hostage soldiers and police officers as goodwill gesture; announcement is made from Cuba, where rebel group is negotiating with government (S)

2000-12-22.title: Mad Cow Angst Dampens Germans' Holidays
2000-12-22.abstract: Germans are suffering great angst this Christmas season as panic over mad cow disease turns them away from their beloved sausages; photo; Germany boasts estimated 1,500 sausage variations and average German consumes about 55 pounds a year, more than 60 percent of total meat consumption; Chancellor Gerhard Schroder issues reassuring statement even as Health Min Andrea Eischer, and environmentalist Green, warns of potential contamination; Austria has banned all German beef and food retailers report surge in poultry prices (M)

2000-12-22.title: Peres Loses Bid to Run for Premiership
2000-12-22.abstract: Shimon Peres loses short-lived bid to run for prime minister of Israel when leftist Meretz Party votes against endorsing him; photo (S)

2000-12-23.title: AFTER LONG FIGHT, U.N. AGREES TO CUT DUES PAID BY U.S.
2000-12-23.abstract: Members of United Nations, after weeks of bitter negotiations, agree in principle to reduce United States dues for first time in quarter century; immediate reaction in Congress is positive, and diplomats say only a few details remain to be settled before General Assembly formally approves accord; chart of US debt to UN since 1980; photo (M)

2000-12-23.title: Bombing at Rome Paper
2000-12-23.abstract: Reported right-wing terrorist sets off bomb outside oB

2000-12-23.title: Rich Nations Will Forgive Debts of 22 Of the Poorest
Tbilisi Journal; Where It's Dark and Cold Outside, and Inside, Too
2000-12-23.abstract: Residents of Tbilisi, Georgia, are perservering through another winter with worsening shortages of electricity and gas for heating; perennial shortages have implications for Pres Eduard Shevardnadze, who has been unable to stop economic slide; so far opposition has remained muted, but small anti-government protests are beginning to erupt in streets of capital; photo (Tbilisi Journal) (M)

2000-12-23.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-12-23.abstract: EUROPE    TURKEY: PRISON SIEGE ENDS -- Soldiers used tear gas to subdue about 400 inmates at a prison in Istanbul, ending five days of raids to reassert government control over 20 prisons across the country. At least 27 people died. The raids were made after hunger strikers refused to end fasts protesting transfers from prisons where up to 100 inmates shared a dormitory-style room to prisons with small cells. Inmates say they fear torture and isolation in the new cells.    Douglas Frantz (NYT)    SPAIN: RUSSIAN GETS BAIL -- A judge ordered Vladimir Gusinsky, left, to be released from jail and confined to his luxury villa in southern Spain under police guard while the Russian media magnate fought Moscow's efforts to extradite him. Judge Baltasar Garzon ruled Mr. Gusinsky must pay $5.55 million in bail to secure his freedom after being detained 10 days ago on an international arrest warrant sought by Russia, where he faces fraud charges.    (Reuters)

2000-12-24.title: Clinton Sends Mideast Sides Back Home With Deadline
2000-12-24.abstract: Five-day search for common ground between Israeli and Palestinian negotiators on toughest issues dividing them ends without breakthrough, and Pres Clinton sends parties home, giving them until Dec 27 to say whether progress could be made (M)

2000-12-24.title: Jerusalem Christians Support Arab Stance
2000-12-24.abstract: The leading Christian churches in Jerusalem have shifted position over the past three months of violence and are backing Palestinian demands for full sovereignty over Jerusalem's Old City and its Christian and Muslim holy sites. Most major Christian faiths in Jerusalem have called for a negotiated solution adhering to United Nations resolutions that require Israeli withdrawal from territory occupied after the 1967 war.    Article, Page 10.

2000-12-24.title: Angola's Goal: Stepping Back From the Abyss
2000-12-24.abstract: Angola, hoping to step back from abyss created by civil war, vows to rebuild its broken cities after decades of war, neglect and squandered riches; government just recently allowed foreign auditors to begin their accounting of country's oil revenue for first time and renews promises to increase spending on crumbling infrastructure, all in effort to secure low-interest loans from West; but measures--which have been praised by State Dept and IMF---only expose magniture of task ahead; country is still crippled by inefficient and corrupt public service, decaying infrastructure and attacks by weakened but unrepentant rebel arm; toll of Angola's civil war is achingly apparent in communities undone by violence, poverty and corruption even as oil exports boom; photos; map (L)

2000-12-24.title: Tinge of Optimism Is Felt in Troubled Kashmir
2000-12-24.abstract: There is sense of optimism in Kashmir region, although India and Pakistan have only agreed to talk bout talking; dispute over Kashmir has been so stubbornly bad that any good news is extraordinary news; map (M)

2000-12-24.title: In China's Heartland, the Fertile Fields Lie Fallow
2000-12-24.abstract: Growing number of Chinese families are letting their farmland lie fallow; in Anhue province and in neighboring regions, from 5 to 15 percent of cropland has recently gone unused, and practice is spreading; abandonment of some of China's most fertile land reflects economic difficulty that has engulfed China in recent years, trapping millions of farm families and leading to frequent reports of rural unrest; these days, China has surplus grain and is phasing out farm subsidies as it prepares to open its markets to global competition; photo; map (L)

2000-12-25.title: Miami Airport Full of Santas, All Heading South
'Fat' Lottery Kicks Off Christmas In Spain
Program Lets Donors Help Pay the Bill to Clear Minefields
2000-12-25.abstract: United Nations Association of United States and Better World Fund, foundation created by Ted Turner, run Adopt-a-Minefield program that enables donors to help pay bill to clear minefields in Asia, Africa and Europe; groups work with State Department and United Nations Development Program, which manages project for UN and its mine-clearing experts (M)

2000-12-25.title: Australia Honors Its Founders (What Were Their Names?)
2000-12-25.abstract: Australia is set to begin year-long celebration marking 100th anniversary of nation's creation from six British colonies; official invitation concedes few Australians know much about nation's founding fathers (M)

2000-12-25.title: Behind Rage In Prisons Of Turkey: Radical Bands
2000-12-25.abstract: Many experts say radical left-wing groups that run prison wards in Turkey like indocrination centers are in part to blame for recent brutal crackdown at 20 prisons; say groups rejected compromises that might have avoided disaster; roots of violence lie in government's lack of control over prisons, routinely run by political groups and criminal gangs as independent fiefs (M)

2000-12-26.title: Daniel Dunham, 71, City Planning Expert
2000-12-26.abstract: Daniel C Dunham, architect and expert on city planning who specialized in designs for tropical areas, dies at age 71 (S)

2000-12-26.title: Fire in Crowded Building Kills At Least 309 in Central China
2000-12-26.abstract: At least 309 people die in Christmas night fire in crowded building in Luoyang, in central China; it is one of worst fires in country's recent history; dozens more people are injured in blaze; poor enforcement of fire codes have led to several devastating fires in China in past few years; country's firefighting equipment is out-of-date and inadequate in many Chinese cities (M)

2000-12-26.title: 6 U.S. Clerics Seek to Safeguard Religious Sites
2000-12-26.abstract: Six American religious leaders ask United Nations General Assembly to declare its support for protection of religious sites around world in age of civil strife; Rabbi Arthur Schneier, who led delegation to UN on behalf of Appeal of Conscience Foundation, says appeal is for all religions (M)

2000-12-26.title: CLINTON PRESENTS A BROAD NEW PLAN FOR MIDEAST PEACE
2000-12-26.abstract: Pres Clinton's new plan for peace accord between Israelis and Palestinians reportedly includes proposal for Israel to cede sovereignty over Temple Mount and for Palestinians to give up right of refugees to return to Israel; plan foresees Palestinian state covering about 95 percent of West Bank; it offers timetable to govern security arrangements over number of years; Clinton's wide-ranging proposals would be very difficult for both sides to accept; they are intended to form basis of comprehensive, end-of-conflict agreement that Clinton is striving to seal before he leaves office on Jan 20; Prime Min Ehud Barak says that proposals would be hard to accept, but that if Yasir Arafat accepts Clinton's plan, Israel would be compelled to accept it as well; Arafat says that he is reviewing plan, but that there are many obstacles (M)

2000-12-26.title: Despite Turkey's Secularism, Ramadan Exerts a Strong Force
2000-12-26.abstract: Tradition of ending day's fast during holy month of Ramadan turns into celebration of religious heritage in Turkey, an avowedly secular country; government is on constant alert for signs of Islamic fundamentalism, and women are forbidden to wear headscarves in universities and public buildings; Religious Affairs Directorate does not estimate how many Turks obey requirement that Muslims fast during Ramadan, but it is clear that great numbers of people are observant (M)

2000-12-27.title: Behind the $100,000 Sable Coat, a Siberian Hunter
2000-12-27.abstract: Russian hunters have become mainstay of world sable production, reversing balance from Soviet times, when sable farming produced majority of pelts; Russia's sable monopoly has helped sustain extensive hunting culture, with at least 10,000 licensed professional hunters and as many as 200,000 amateurs; sables from Siberia's Barguzin region are most prized; map; photos (M)

2000-12-27.title: Mexico Allows Volcano Evacuees to Return
2000-12-27.abstract: MEXICO CITY, Dec. 26 — Authorities gave tens of thousands of people permission to return to their homes near the base of the Popocatepetl volcano today, but warned them to remain on alert.

2000-12-27.title: Boy's Death Underscores Britain's Urban Blight
2000-12-27.abstract: London's Peckham neighborhood has been focus of attention since Nov 27 murder of Damilola Taylor, 10, by group of youths at housing complex; Conservative leader, William Hague, has used case to attack government's approach to law enforcement, holding anti-racism policies bar police from doing their job in places with high minority populations, like Peckham; youth's family, who came from Nigeria four months ago, has denounced Hague's remarks; area residents are in shock, both from murder and from ensuing media onslaught; photo (M)

2000-12-27.title: Where Darwin Mused, Strife Over Ecosystem
2000-12-27.abstract: Fisherman unwilling to accept limits on their catch are openly and violently defying Ecuador's efforts to preserve Galapagos Islands' delicate and threatened ecosystem; have attacked conservation installations, blockaded ports and harassed tourist groups; are protesting 1998 law that gave islands' residents greater autonomy and set up marine reserve out to 40 miles offshore; photos (M)

2000-12-27.title: Young Asian Knifed by German Neo-Nazis
2000-12-27.abstract: Rightist youths shouting anti-immigrant insult stabbed and seriously wound young man of 'Asian appearance,' Guben, Germany, scene of 1999 killing of young Algerian by xenophobic mob; police arrest three young men in what they call racist attack (M)

2000-12-28.title: Bush Could Get Tougher on Venezuela's Leader
2000-12-28.abstract: Republican officials and foreign policy analysts expect administration of George W Bush to take tougher stand against Pres Hugo Chavez of Venezuela; Republicans believe Chavez is undercutting American foreign policy by providing oil to Cuba, opposing US counter-narcotics aid to Colombia, and giving political support to guerrillas and anti-government forces in neighboring Andean nations; there is also concern that Chavez is distorting democratic free-market model advocated in Washington by consolidating institutions under his control and setting himself up as elected dictator; there is no bipartisan consensus on whether Chavez is merely nuisance or real threat to United States interests in Latin America (M)

2000-12-28.title: 3 ARAB COUNTRIES REACT FAVORABLY TO MIDEAST PLAN
2000-12-28.abstract: Leaders of Egypt, Jordan and Saudi Arabia said to react favorably to Pres Clinton's proposed Middle East peace settlement; all voice support for concepts in plan, which essentially envisions giving Palestinians full control over Temple Mount and Arab neighborhoods in East Jerusalem, in exchange for Palestinian agreement to forgo demand that all Palestinian refugees be allowed to return to Israel; Prime Min Ehud Barak of Israel has announced that Israel is prepared to accept proposals if Palestinians accept them; Palestinian side has not issued clear-cut reply, forwarding instead list of questions and objections; Clinton hopes that support from leaders of Jordan, Egypt and Saudi Arabia will persuade Yasir Arafat to agree to proposal (M)

2000-12-28.title: German Bid for Dominance Resisted by French : Will Gaullist Grandeur Obstruct a New Europe?
2000-12-28.abstract: PARIS—  There is a worst-case theory for Europe in which France becomes its engine of divisiveness, a nation so unwilling to redefine itself that it blocks the way to European development with an obstinate defense of its own aging myths and particularities.

2000-12-28.title: Louis Leprince-Ringuet, French Physicist, 99
2000-12-28.abstract: Prof Louis Leprince-Ringuet, French nuclear physicist who was the first to spot evidence of a new type of subatomic particle, the meson, dies at age 99; photo (M)

2000-12-28.title: Indonesia Bombings May Fuel Regional Instability : Tension in Southeast Asia
2000-12-28.abstract: SINGAPORE—  A bombing campaign designed to provoke violence in Indonesia and destabilize the government could inflame religious and ethnic tensions elsewhere in Southeast Asia, officials and analysts warn.

2000-12-29.title: Rio Journal; For Brazil's College-Bound, a Brutal Test of Mettle
2000-12-29.abstract: Comment on dreadful right-of-passage for Brazilian high school students; graduates who want to go on to college are forced to prove themselves in series of exhausting, ferociously competitive and all-important college entrance examinations; photos (L)

2000-12-29.title: Static Screens of Czech TV Mirror Deep Political Problem
2000-12-29.abstract: Dispute over new director blacks out state television this week and brings thousands of demonstrators into streets, drags in Pres Vaclav Havel and raises serious questions about Czech Republic's politics as it pushes to join European Union; photo (M)

2000-12-29.title: Clinton Scraps North Korea Trip, Saying Time's Short for Deal
2000-12-29.abstract: Pres Clinton says he has decided not to visit North Korea before end of his term because he lacks time to put together a deal ending its nuclear missile program (M)

2000-12-29.title: . . . as Killings Go On
2000-12-29.abstract: Prime Minister Ehud Barak of Israel ordered the army to cordon off the entire West Bank and Gaza Strip after bombings that killed two Israelis, even as the Palestinian and Israeli leaders plotted out their continued responses to a peace initiative.    Article, Page A10.

2000-12-29.title: Argentina Plans to Allot Public Works $20 Billion
2000-12-29.abstract: Argentine govt officials announce plans to spend $20 billion on public works programs, to jump-start economy that has been slumping for two years (M)

2000-12-31.title: As U.S. Military Settles In, Some in Ecuador Have Doubts
2000-12-31.abstract: United States is spending $62 million to modernize base at Manta, Ecuador, including construction of living quarters for 200 American military and civilian contract personnel, as important element in effort to halt drug trafficking; 'forward operating location,' as Manta is called, will provide round-the-clock tracking of activity in major coca-growing areas in nearby Colombia, using Awacs surveillance planes; American military officials are sensitive to Ecuadorean concerns about sovereignty; photo; map (M)

2000-12-31.title: South Africa Looks West for Some Economic Muscle
2000-12-31.abstract: South Africa is seeking allies in free-trade fight that began when World Trade Orgn met year ago in Seattle; it is looking to India, to Egypt, and especially to Brazil; it has agreed to begin talks on joining Mercosur, six-country South American trading bloc anchored by Brazil; little trade currently crosses Atlantic between South Africa and its prospective partners; even if agreement flourishes, South America could not supplant United States or European Union as more active trading partner; but South Africa is envisioning new era in trade, in which South America would be strategically essential partner (M)

2000-12-31.title: A Fit City Offers Russia a Self-Help Model
2000-12-31.abstract: Last article in series about Russian health care, Freedom's Toll, examines how city of Dubna could be textbook example of how all of Russia could heal itself; medical care in Dubna has been transformed in last decade through remarkable partnership between Dubna and American city of La Crosse, Wis; thousand Wisconsinites have traveled to Russia to pore over Dubna's health-care system, and been host to thousand Dubna residents in turn; Dubna has rebuilt maternity hospital, kidney dialysis center and diabetes education center; it has women's wellness clinics and rehabilitation center for disabled children and adults; United States Agency for International Development program that subsidizes partnerships between American health care institutions and former Soviet cities has provided nearly $1 million; American participants in program credit Dubna's mayor, Valery Eduardovich Prokh, for success of program, saying he is intolerant of obstacles to change, and determined to make life better for people of Dubna; photos; map (L)

2000-12-31.title: After a Year, Millennium Dome Calls It a Day
2000-12-31.abstract: Britain's Millennium Dome, which opened with great fanfare, will close; beleaguered project began encountering problems opening night; Prime Min Tony Blair, who initially predicted Dome would become 'envy of the world,' is nowhere to be seen when announcement is made, and one British columnist notes how government has labored throughout year to avoid assuming responsibility for $1.2 billion debacle (M)

2000-12-31.title: Crusader Hopes to Put Aggression on World List of War Crimes
2000-12-31.abstract: Benjamin B Ferencz, member of American prosecution team at Nuremberg trials after World War II, wants aggression to be declared war crime; he is disdainful of those who reject his mission, who claim there is no clear definition of aggression; he is particularly distressed by reluctance of his adopted country, United States, and Clinton administration to be part of International Criminal Court as that moves closer to reality (M)
